name: Trophy Dark
edition: Trophy Dark (Jesse Ross, 2019 — Codex publication; mechanics descend from Jason Tocci's 24XX SRD)
license: Trophy Dark mechanics by Jesse Ross / Hedgemaze Press. 24XX SRD (Jason Tocci) is CC BY 4.0. This ruleset file is a clean-room re-implementation of the resolution mechanic for the Marinara-RPG framework; no setting prose, treasure-hunter pitches, ruin descriptions, or other Hedgemaze copyrightable text is reproduced. Buy the game at hedgemazepress.com.
summary: One-shot horror RPG: treasure hunters in a haunted wood, doomed from the start. Each character has Pursuits (light-die professions) and Backgrounds (light-die histories). Risk rolls mix the character's light d6s with situational dark d6s; the highest light face determines outcome (5+ clean, 4 cost, 1-3 devil's bargain), and any dark die higher than the highest light die inflicts Ruin. APPROXIMATED here as a stance-modal-pool: stance=light succeeds OVER Risk (3) to mirror the 4+ band, stance=dark fails UNDER Risk to mirror the corruption-overrules-light cascade. See gm-agent.md for the deviation note — true Trophy Dark wants a dual-pool with comparison, which stance-modal-pool cannot express in one roll.

dice.notation: Pd6 stance=LIGHT|DARK vs Risk (light dice = your Pursuits + Backgrounds; dark dice = GM-assigned)

stances.label: DARK
stances.description: Roll the GM-assigned dark dice for this scene. A dark die UNDER Risk (1-2) is mundane menace; faces 4-6 represent the wood's corruption gaining ground. Use the gm-agent's dark-overrule guidance when narrating, since stance-modal-pool cannot express 'highest dark beats highest light' in a single roll.

stances.label: LIGHT
stances.description: Roll your character's light dice (1 die per applicable Pursuit, +1 per applicable Background, +1 per Devil's Bargain accepted, +1 per Helping ally). A face OVER Risk (4-6) is a success; the outcomeTier walks this success count to clean / costly / devil's bargain.

exactMatch.name: EDGE OF RUIN
exactMatch.effect: A die rolling EXACTLY Risk (3) is a hair's-breadth moment. It does NOT count as success on its own, but on a light roll the GM should flavor the narration with 'the wood almost takes you' — a free reminder that Ruin is coming. On a dark roll, it counts as a near-miss the GM banks for the next scene.

outcomeTiers.label: devils-bargain
outcomeTiers.narration: No light die came up 4+. The action fails as attempted; GM offers a Devil's Bargain (mark Ruin, accept a complication, sacrifice an item, or trade something the wood wants) to convert this into a costly success. Decline and the failure stands. Trophy Dark RAW: tier 1-3 on highest light.

outcomeTiers.label: at-a-cost
outcomeTiers.narration: Exactly one light die came up 4+ — and likely it was the 4 itself. You succeed AT A COST: harm, complication, a piece of yourself left in the wood. Now check dark dice (GM): any dark face higher than the highest light face? If yes, mark Ruin. Trophy Dark RAW: tier 4 on highest light.

outcomeTiers.label: clean
outcomeTiers.narration: Multiple light dice came up 4+ — at least one was likely a 5 or 6. The action succeeds cleanly. Even so, check dark dice: if any dark face exceeded your highest light face, mark Ruin anyway. The wood corrupts even clean wins. Trophy Dark RAW: tier 5-6 on highest light.

attributes.name: Risk
attributes.description: Fixed Trophy Dark threshold (3). Faces 4-6 succeed on light dice; faces 1-3 fail. Held at min=max=3 to model the system's invariant Risk line — Trophy Dark does not vary this stat per character (unlike L&F's Number). Player should not edit.

skills.name: Burglar
skills.description: Pursuit. Climbing, picking locks, slipping into places that don't want you. When the GM agrees this Pursuit applies, contribute 1 light die to the Risk roll.

skills.name: Brute
skills.description: Pursuit. Strength, intimidation, smashing through obstacles. Contributes 1 light die when applicable.

skills.name: Sneak
skills.description: Pursuit. Stealth, deception, going unseen. Contributes 1 light die when applicable.

skills.name: Scholar
skills.description: Pursuit. Lore, languages, reading the unreadable. Contributes 1 light die when applicable.

skills.name: Cursed
skills.description: Background. A doom rides on your shoulders. When the curse is relevant (often: when something supernatural blocks the path), contribute 1 light die to the Risk roll.

skills.name: Doomed
skills.description: Background. You know how you die — you've seen the wood-shape of it. Contributes 1 light die when the doom-vision applies.

skills.name: Haunted
skills.description: Background. Something dead follows you. Contributes 1 light die when communing with the dead, surviving fear, or facing the wood's spirits.

skills.name: Soldier
skills.description: Background. You bled in a war and came home wrong. Contributes 1 light die for combat, command, and reading other violent people.

skills.name: Smuggler
skills.description: Background. Routes, contacts, the trick of moving forbidden things. Contributes 1 light die for navigation, contraband, and lies told fast.

derivedStats.name: Ruin
derivedStats.formula: ticks accumulated from failed Risk rolls and Devil's Bargains; at Ruin = 6 the character is consumed by the wood

derivedStats.name: Burdens
derivedStats.formula: narrative tracker for accepted Devil's Bargains the GM is holding over the character; not a number that drives dice math

diceTagFormat.template: [mrrp-roll: ruleset=trophy-dark, stance={stance}, stat=Risk, statValue={statValue}, pool={pool}, dice=[{diceCsv}], successes={successes}, exactMatches={exactMatches}, tier={tier}{narrationHookPart}]
diceTagFormat.example: [mrrp-roll: ruleset=trophy-dark, stance=light, stat=Risk, statValue=3, pool=3, dice=[2,4,5], successes=2, exactMatches=0, tier=clean, narrationHook=]
